llm_verdict.vuln_description: The system failed to bind execution approvals to a specific node ID, allowing an attacker to intercept an approval intended for one node and replay it to execute unauthorized commands on a different node.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: approving system.run requests without validating the target nodeId against the approval scope
llm_verdict.causal_chain: The commit established the ExecApprovalRequest data structure without a nodeId field, creating a design flaw where approvals were node-agnostic and could be replayed across any node in the system.

cves.description: OpenClaw: Native prompt image auto-load did not honor tools.fs.workspaceOnly in sandboxed runs

cves.description: OpenClaw: system.run approval identity mismatch could execute a different binary than displayed
cves.how_introduced: The system.run approval matching relied on a rendered command string that did not preserve exact argv identity (e.g., whitespace), allowing an attacker to swap the executable path by appending spaces and reuse an existing approval for a different binary.

llm_verdict.causal_chain: The blamed commit defined `ExecApprovalRequest` with only a `command` string field. Because the exact `argv` array was not preserved in the request, the system had to match approvals based on the rendered string, allowing attackers to bypass authorization by appending spaces to swap the executable path.

cves.description: OpenClaw's sandboxed sessions_spawn now enforces sandbox inheritance for cross-agent spawns

cves.description: OpenClaw has a Trusted-proxy Control UI pairing bypass which allows unpaired node sessions

cves.description: Shakapacker has environment variable leak via EnvironmentPlugin that exposes secrets to client-side bundles

The suspected commit (591e3de9f1113a7d2f131315622b41588682d08a) extracts the `getPlugins` function from `package/environments/base.js` into `package/plugins/webpack.js` and `package/plugins/rspack.js`.
The diff of `package/environments/base.js` in the suspected commit shows that `new webpack.EnvironmentPlugin(process.env)` was already present in the file before the commit.

The blamed file `package/plugins/rspack.ts` (evolved from `rspack.js`) was entirely new code introduced by this commit. While the same vulnerability pattern already existed in the webpack code path (in `base.js`), the rspack-specific instance of this vulnerability was introduced by this commit. The fix explicitly patches this exact line in `rspack.ts`.
evidence: The BIC creates `package/plugins/rspack.js` as a new file containing `new rspack.EnvironmentPlugin(process.env)` on line 12
The fix commit replaces `new rspack.EnvironmentPlugin(process.env)` with `new rspack.EnvironmentPlugin(getFilteredEnv())` in `package/plugins/rspack.ts`

The same vulnerable pattern `new webpack.EnvironmentPlugin(process.env)` already existed in `package/environments/base.js` before this commit, but the BIC also moved it to the new `package/plugins/webpack.js`

cves.description: yt-dlp: Arbitrary Command Injection when using the `--netrc-cmd` option
cves.how_introduced: The `_get_netrc_login_info` method fails to validate the `netrc_machine` variable before passing it to a shell command via the `--netrc-cmd` option, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary shell commands if they can control the machine name.

bug_commits.author: Nicolai Dagestad
bug_commits.message: Add option `--netrc-cmd` (#6682)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit introduces the `--netrc-cmd` feature, which formats the `netrc_machine` variable directly into a shell command and executes it using `Popen.run` with `shell=True`. This directly creates the command injection vulnerability that the fix addresses by adding character validation.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The `_get_netrc_login_info` method fails to validate the `netrc_machine` variable before passing it to a shell command via the `--netrc-cmd` option, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary shell commands if they can control the machine name.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: subprocess.check_output(f'{cmd} {netrc_machine}', shell=True)
llm_verdict.causal_chain: The blamed commit added the `netrc_cmd` execution logic in `_get_netrc_login_info`. By passing the unvalidated `netrc_machine` string into a command executed with `shell=True`, it allowed arbitrary command injection if an attacker could control the `netrc_machine` value.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: This commit introduced the vulnerable `--netrc-cmd` feature and its unsafe implementation in `_get_netrc_login_info`. Specifically, it formats the user-controlled `netrc_machine` into the command string and executes it with `shell=True`, which is exactly what the fix later hardens by validating `netrc_machine` before command construction/execution. The other fix hunk around `_perform_login` only limits when login is attempted; the security-relevant remediation is the new validation of `netrc_machine`, addressing the injection path created here.
evidence: Commit message is `Add option --netrc-cmd (#6682)`, showing this commit introduced the feature.
This commit added `cmd = self.get_param('netrc_cmd', '').format(netrc_machine)` followed by `Popen.run(cmd, text=True, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE)` in `yt_dlp/extractor/common.py`.

The fix later adds validation rejecting missing/invalid `netrc_machine` and disallowing leading `-`/`_` and non `[A-Za-z0-9._-]` characters before using it in `--netrc-cmd`, directly mitigating the injection vector introduced here.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: This commit introduced the `--netrc-cmd` feature, which formats `netrc_machine` directly into a shell command string via `.format(netrc_machine)` and executes it with `Popen.run(cmd, text=True, shell=True, ...)`. The `netrc_machine` value was not sanitized, allowing arbitrary command injection if a malicious value was used. The fix commit directly addresses this by adding character validation for `netrc_machine` before it can be interpolated into the command. Before this commit, no shell command execution involving `netrc_machine` existed, so the vulnerability could not have been present.
evidence: The BIC introduces the line: `cmd = self.get_param('netrc_cmd', '').format(netrc_machine)` which formats unsanitized netrc_machine into a command string
The BIC executes this command with `Popen.run(cmd, text=True, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE)` allowing shell injection

The fix commit adds validation: `ALLOWED = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ0123456789.-_'` and checks `not all(c in ALLOWED for c in netrc_machine)` before the command is constructed
Before this commit, there was no --netrc-cmd option and no shell command execution involving netrc_machine

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The suspected commit introduces the `--netrc-cmd` feature, which allows users to specify a command to fetch netrc credentials. In doing so, it formats the `netrc_machine` variable into the command string and executes it using `shell=True`. Because `netrc_machine` can be influenced by the extractor (and potentially by a crafted URL), an attacker can inject shell metacharacters into `netrc_machine` to execute arbitrary commands. The fix commit addresses this exact issue by validating `netrc_machine` against an allowlist of safe characters before it is used in the command. Therefore, the suspected commit is the bug-introducing commit.
evidence: The suspected commit `db3ad8a67661d7b234a6954d9c6a4a9b1749f5eb` introduces the `--netrc-cmd` option and the `_get_netrc_login_info` logic that executes the command.
The suspected commit formats `netrc_machine` into the command string and executes it with `shell=True` via `Popen.run`.
The fix commit `1fbbe29b99dc61375bf6d786f824d9fcf6ea9c1a` mitigates the vulnerability by validating `netrc_machine` to ensure it only contains safe characters, preventing shell injection.

cves.description: Mattermost Desktop App versions <6.0.0 fail to sanitize sensitive information from Mattermost logs and clear data on server deletion which allows an attacker with access to the users system to gain access to potentially sensitive information via reading the application logs.
cves.how_introduced: The application fails to sanitize sensitive data before writing to local logs and does not properly clear application data when a server is deleted, potentially exposing credentials or session info to local attackers.

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit introduced the file 'mainWindow.ts' and implemented the logging statements that were identified as vulnerable. By creating the initial logging infrastructure without sanitization, it established the pattern of logging raw objects (like windowOptions, reason, and matchingScreen) that led to the information exposure vulnerability.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The application fails to sanitize sensitive data before writing to local logs and does not properly clear application data when a server is deleted, potentially exposing credentials or session info to local attackers.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: Logging of sensitive application state or user data without sanitization and lack of data cleanup on server removal.
llm_verdict.causal_chain: The commit introduced the MainWindow class and associated logging calls using raw objects, which lacked sanitization for sensitive data, thereby creating the vulnerability that was later addressed by wrapping these objects in braces to prevent direct logging of sensitive state.

cves.description: Mattermost Confluence Plugin has Missing Authorization vulnerability
cves.how_introduced: The plugin fails to properly authenticate or authorize users before allowing them to perform critical functions like saving or editing subscriptions.

bug_commits.author: kshitij katiyar
bug_commits.message: Check Confluence access for subscription and config checks (#155)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit removed explicit connection checks that verified if a user was connected to Confluence before proceeding with the subscription edit. By deleting this logic, the code lost its primary mechanism for ensuring the user had an active, authorized session, leading to the missing authorization vulnerability.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The plugin fails to properly authenticate or authorize users before allowing them to perform critical functions like saving or editing subscriptions.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: Missing or improperly implemented authentication/authorization checks before executing sensitive operations.
llm_verdict.causal_chain: The commit removed the block that loaded the user's connection from the store and verified the existence of a ConfluenceAccountID, effectively bypassing the authentication requirement for the subscription editing process.

cves.description: HomeBox Missing User Authorization
cves.how_introduced: The GitHub Actions workflow directly interpolates untrusted context variables like `${{ github.event.before }}` and `${{ github.event.after }}` into a bash script, allowing for potential command injection.

bug_commits.author: Matt Kilgore
bug_commits.message: Remove documents table (#618)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit introduced several functions (AttachmentPath, AttachmentUpdate, AttachmentDelete) that performed database operations using only an attachmentID, without verifying the user's group ID (GID). The fix commit corrects this by adding the 'gid' parameter to these functions and passing it to the repository layer to ensure proper authorization/scoping.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The GitHub Actions workflow directly interpolates untrusted context variables like `${{ github.event.before }}` and `${{ github.event.after }}` into a bash script, allowing for potential command injection.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: CHANGED_FILES=$(git diff --name-only ${{ github.event.before }}..${{ github.event.after }})
llm_verdict.causal_chain:
1. The blamed commit added/refactored attachment management functions in the service layer.
2. These functions were implemented to retrieve or modify data using only the object's UUID (attachmentID).
3. This created a missing authorization vulnerability where any authenticated user could potentially access or modify attachments belonging to other groups by guessing the UUID.

cves.description: Stirling-PDF SSRF vulnerability on /api/v1/convert/html/pdf
cves.how_introduced: The application allowed user-provided URLs or HTML content to be processed by conversion services without validating the destination, enabling attackers to make requests to internal network resources.

bug_commits.author: Balázs Szücs
bug_commits.message: Add EML to PDF conversion functionality (#3650)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit introduced the EmlToPdf utility class, which established the initial logic for processing EML files into PDFs. This implementation lacked the necessary HTML sanitization mechanisms, creating the vulnerable pattern where unsanitized content was passed to conversion services, which the fix commit later addressed by injecting a CustomHtmlSanitizer.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The application allowed user-provided URLs or HTML content to be processed by conversion services without validating the destination, enabling attackers to make requests to internal network resources.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: Passing unsanitized user-provided URLs or HTML content directly to PDF conversion libraries (e.g., wkhtmltopdf or similar) that perform network requests.
llm_verdict.causal_chain: The blamed commit created the EmlToPdf utility and its conversion pipeline without any sanitization logic, directly enabling the processing of unsanitized HTML content that leads to SSRF when handled by the underlying PDF conversion library.

cves.description: MobSF Path Traversal in GET /download/<filename> using absolute filenames
cves.how_introduced: The application failed to properly validate user-supplied filenames, allowing attackers to use absolute paths or traversal sequences to access files outside the intended directory.

bug_commits.author: Ajin Abraham
bug_commits.message: June 22nd 2025 updates (#2530)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit introduced the `get_script_content` function which constructs file paths using user-supplied input from `request.POST.getlist('scripts[]')`. It implemented an insufficient `is_safe_path` check that only validated the base directory and the final path, but failed to pass the raw user input for proper validation against absolute paths or traversal sequences.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The application failed to properly validate user-supplied filenames, allowing attackers to use absolute paths or traversal sequences to access files outside the intended directory.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: os.path.join(base_dir, user_input) without sufficient validation of user_input for absolute paths or traversal sequences
llm_verdict.causal_chain: 1. The blamed commit added the `get_script_content` function. 2. This function takes user-controlled input from the 'scripts[]' POST parameter. 3. It joins this input with a base directory using `others / f'{script}.js'`. 4. It calls `is_safe_path` with only the base and the resulting path, which was insufficient to catch certain traversal or absolute path attacks until the fix added the raw user input as a third argument.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The fix for this issue is the addition of a third argument to is_safe_path in get_script_content, indicating the original validation was insufficient for attacker-controlled script names. The suspected commit is exactly the one that introduced get_script_content and its vulnerable check `is_safe_path(str(others), str(script_file))` while constructing `script_file` from untrusted `scripts[]` input. Since this file did not exist before and the vulnerable logic was added wholesale in this commit, it is the bug-introducing commit for this path-traversal flaw.
evidence: Commit 7e0355c51d78d9911b5f8722f306d182f9975c35 added new file mobsf/DynamicAnalyzer/views/common/frida/views.py containing get_script_content.
In that new function, user-controlled `script` values from `request.POST.getlist('scripts[]')` are appended into a path and only checked with `is_safe_path(str(others), str(script_file))`.
The later fix commit changes exactly that line to `is_safe_path(str(others), str(script_file), script)`, showing the original two-argument validation was the security bug.
The vulnerable endpoint logic did not exist before this commit because the file was newly created in 7e0355c51d78d9911b5f8722f306d182f9975c35.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The CVE (CVE-2025-58161) is about path traversal in GET /download/<filename> using absolute filenames, primarily affecting the `download` function in `home.py`. The BIC commit (7e0355c5) did not touch `home.py` at all. It only refactored frida views by moving code from `frida.py` to `frida/views.py`, carrying over the same vulnerable `is_safe_path(others, script_file)` pattern that already existed in the old file. The vulnerability in `is_safe_path` (not checking for absolute paths in raw user input) pre-existed this commit. This commit merely relocated already-vulnerable code during a refactoring.
evidence: The CVE description says 'Path Traversal in GET /download/<filename> using absolute filenames' - this is in home.py which was NOT modified by the BIC
The BIC moved code from frida.py to frida/views.py - the old frida.py already had the same vulnerable pattern: `if not is_safe_path(others, script_file):`
The core vulnerability is in `is_safe_path` function in utils.py which lacked absolute path checking - this function was not modified by the BIC

The fix commit modifies `is_safe_path` to accept a `raw_file` parameter and check `is_path_traversal(raw_file)` which detects absolute paths - this is a pre-existing design flaw in the utility function

agent_verdicts.reasoning: Fallback verdict: Agent exhausted max turns without submitting a verdict.

cves.description: MobSF Vulnerable to Arbitrary File Write (AR-Slip) via Absolute Path in .a Extraction
cves.how_introduced: The application failed to properly validate user-supplied filenames, allowing attackers to use absolute paths or traversal sequences to access files outside the intended directory.

bug_commits.author: Ajin Abraham
bug_commits.message: June 22nd 2025 updates (#2530)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit introduced the 'get_script_content' function and the exact vulnerable line where 'is_safe_path' is called without the necessary third argument to properly validate the user-supplied script name.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The application failed to properly validate user-supplied filenames, allowing attackers to use absolute paths or traversal sequences to access files outside the intended directory.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: os.path.join(base_dir, user_input) without sufficient validation of user_input for absolute paths or traversal sequences
llm_verdict.causal_chain: The blamed commit created the file and added the 'get_script_content' function, which constructs a file path using user input ('script') and validates it using 'is_safe_path(str(others), str(script_file))'. Because it omitted the user input itself from the validation check, it allowed path traversal, leading to the vulnerability.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The fix works by changing this call site from `is_safe_path(base, path)` to `is_safe_path(base, path, script)`, because `is_safe_path` had been strengthened to reject absolute paths and traversal only when given the raw user input. In the bug-introducing commit, `get_script_content` was newly added and passed only the joined path, so attacker-controlled absolute paths in `script` could bypass validation after path joining. Since this file and vulnerable call were introduced in 7e0355c5, that commit is the origin of the vulnerability in this code path.
evidence: Commit 7e0355c5 newly introduced `mobsf/DynamicAnalyzer/views/common/frida/views.py` and the vulnerable code: `script_file = others / f'{script}.js'` followed by `is_safe_path(str(others), str(script_file))`.
At commit 7e0355c5, `is_safe_path` accepted only `(safe_root, check_path)` and merely compared normalized real paths; it did not inspect the raw filename for absolute-path/traversal tricks.
Later hardening in `mobsf/MobSF/utils.py` changed `is_safe_path` to require a third parameter `raw_file` and reject path traversal/absolute paths via `is_path_traversal(raw_file)`.

The security fix in 7f3bc086 updated this exact call site to pass `script` as the third argument: `is_safe_path(str(others), str(script_file), script)`, directly addressing the bypass in code introduced by 7e0355c5.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The CVE (CVE-2025-58162) is specifically about "Arbitrary File Write (AR-Slip) via Absolute Path in .a Extraction." The core vulnerability is in the `ar_os()` function in `shared_func.py`, which extracts AR archives without checking for path traversal in file entries. The suspected BIC (7e0355c) created `frida/views.py` but did NOT modify `shared_func.py` or the `ar_os` function at all. While the fix commit also updated the `is_safe_path` call in `frida/views.py` (changing from 2 to 3 arguments), this is a secondary hardening change, not the core AR-Slip vulnerability described in the CVE. The `ar_os` function's lack of path traversal checking predates this commit.

The core fix is in ar_os() in shared_func.py, adding is_path_traversal() checks on AR file entries before extraction
The suspected BIC (7e0355c) did not modify shared_func.py at all - confirmed by empty diff
The ar_os() function existed before this commit with the same vulnerable code (no path traversal check)
The frida/views.py change in the fix (is_safe_path 2->3 args) is a secondary hardening, not the core AR-Slip vulnerability

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The vulnerability described by CVE-2025-58162 is an Arbitrary File Write (AR-Slip) via Absolute Path in .a Extraction. The fix for this specific vulnerability is located in `mobsf/StaticAnalyzer/views/common/shared_func.py` within the `ar_os` function, where it adds checks for path traversal in the extracted files. The fix commit addresses multiple security issues at once, including the AR-Slip vulnerability and a separate path traversal issue in the Frida scripts endpoint. bug_commits.author: Jaeyou PARK
bug_commits.message: fix(ios_analyzer): Correctly resolve executable path in .app bundles (#2533)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit introduced the logic that constructs the binary path using Path objects derived from user-supplied input (src). By replacing the previous iteration logic with a glob search that directly assigns the path to bin_dir and subsequently joins it with bin_name, it created the vulnerable path construction pattern that lacks validation against traversal or absolute paths.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The application failed to properly validate user-supplied filenames, allowing attackers to use absolute paths or traversal sequences to access files outside the intended directory.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: os.path.join(base_dir, user_input) without sufficient validation of user_input for absolute paths or traversal sequences
llm_verdict.causal_chain: The commit replaced manual directory traversal with a glob-based path resolution that directly uses the result of Path(src).glob('**/*.app') to define bin_dir. This allows an attacker to influence the path construction, leading to the arbitrary file write vulnerability when bin_path is later used for file operations.

bug_commits.pr_title: fix(ios_analyzer): Correctly resolve executable path in .app bundles

cves.description: BunnyPad Vulnerable to Buffer Overflow When Opening Files of Size 20MB or Greater
cves.how_introduced: The application reads entire files into memory without checking their size, leading to memory exhaustion or crashes when opening files larger than 20MB.

bug_commits.author: Garry Strait
bug_commits.message: Push Local Changes - Aug 13 @ 02:10 EDT (#27)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit (12d0e719ef16) represents a massive rewrite and expansion of the v11-PyQt6.py file, introducing the core application structure and the file-handling logic that lacks size validation. The fix commit (d9224eb5e13c) modifies the code structure established in this blamed commit to finally implement the necessary safety checks.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The application reads entire files into memory without checking their size, leading to memory exhaustion or crashes when opening files larger than 20MB.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: file.read() without size limits or chunking
llm_verdict.causal_chain: The blamed commit introduced the file-reading implementation (likely using file.read() without constraints) as part of the new PyQt6 application architecture, establishing the vulnerable pattern that remained unvalidated until the fix commit.

cves.description: quic-go HTTP/3 QPACK Header Expansion DoS
cves.how_introduced: The HTTP/3 implementation failed to enforce a limit on the total size of decompressed QPACK headers, allowing an attacker to send highly compressed headers that expand into a large amount of memory, leading to resource exhaustion.

bug_commits.author: Marten Seemann
bug_commits.message: update qpack to v0.6.0 (#5434)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit introduced the streaming-based header decoding pattern using `decodeFn()`, which removed the ability to inspect the full header set before processing. By switching to an iterative decoding loop without implementing a size limit, the commit created the vulnerability where headers could be expanded indefinitely without validation.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The HTTP/3 implementation failed to enforce a limit on the total size of decompressed QPACK headers, allowing an attacker to send highly compressed headers that expand into a large amount of memory, leading to resource exhaustion.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: requestFromHeaders(decodeFromSlice(headers), nil)
llm_verdict.causal_chain: The commit replaced a slice-based header input with a streaming `decodeFn` loop, which processes headers one by one without tracking the cumulative size, thereby enabling the resource exhaustion vulnerability.

bug_commits.message: refactor: port reqparse to BaseModel (#28993)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit introduced the ConversationRenamePayload class with the 'name' and 'auto_generate' fields but without the necessary validation logic. This directly created the vulnerability where empty or null names could be accepted when auto_generate is false.

cves.description: Mattermost Confluence Plugin has Missing Authorization vulnerability
cves.how_introduced: The plugin fails to properly authenticate or authorize users before allowing them to perform critical functions like saving or editing subscriptions.

bug_commits.author: kshitij katiyar
bug_commits.message: Check Confluence access for subscription and config checks (#155)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit removed explicit connection checks that verified if a user was connected to Confluence before proceeding with the subscription edit. By deleting this logic, the code lost its primary mechanism for ensuring the user had an active, authorized session, leading to the missing authorization vulnerability.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The plugin fails to properly authenticate or authorize users before allowing them to perform critical functions like saving or editing subscriptions.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: Missing or improperly implemented authentication/authorization checks before executing sensitive operations.
llm_verdict.causal_chain: The commit removed the block that loaded the user's connection from the store and verified the existence of a ConfluenceAccountID, effectively bypassing the authentication requirement for the subscription editing process.

cves.description: FrankenPHP affected by Path Confusion via Unicode casing in CGI path splitting allows execution of arbitrary files
cves.how_introduced: The worker mode in FrankenPHP failed to properly isolate or reset session state between consecutive requests, leading to cross-request session data leakage.

bug_commits.author: Kévin Dunglas
bug_commits.message: feat: hot reload (#2031)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit introduced the pattern of storing a single request option pointer (`f.mercureHubRequestOption = &opt`) at the module level instead of appending it to a request-scoped slice. This caused the Mercure hub configuration to persist or be improperly managed across different requests in worker mode, leading to the session/state isolation vulnerability.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The worker mode in FrankenPHP failed to properly isolate or reset session state between consecutive requests, leading to cross-request session data leakage.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: dependency on vulnerable version of github.com/dunglas/frankenphp (v1.11.1) which lacks proper request-scoped session isolation
llm_verdict.causal_chain: The blamed commit replaced a local assignment with a module-level state assignment (f.mercureHubRequestOption = &opt) -> This shared state was not properly isolated between requests in worker mode -> The fix commit resolved this by moving to a slice-based 'requestOptions' approach to ensure proper scoped handling.

cves.description: ActualBudget server is Missing Authentication for SimpleFIN and Pluggy AI bank sync endpoints
cves.how_introduced: The SimpleFIN and Pluggy AI bank synchronization endpoints were accessible without proper authentication, allowing unauthorized access to sensitive financial integration features.

bug_commits.author: Zach Whelchel
bug_commits.message: SimpleFin (#296)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit introduces the SimpleFIN application routes (`/status`, `/accounts`, `/transactions`) without including any authentication middleware. The fix commit later adds `validateSessionMiddleware` to secure these endpoints.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The SimpleFIN and Pluggy AI bank synchronization endpoints were accessible without proper authentication, allowing unauthorized access to sensitive financial integration features.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: Missing authentication middleware or decorators on bank synchronization API routes
llm_verdict.causal_chain: The blamed commit created the `app-simplefin.js` file and defined the API endpoints for SimpleFIN integration. By omitting authentication checks (like `validateSessionMiddleware`) during the initial implementation, it allowed unauthorized access to these sensitive endpoints.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The fix for CVE-2026-27584 is the addition of `validateSessionMiddleware` to the SimpleFIN Express app, which enforces authentication on all SimpleFIN endpoints. In the suspected commit, the SimpleFIN app is introduced as a new file with `app.use(express.json())` and unauthenticated `POST /status`, `/accounts`, and `/transactions` handlers, but no session-validation middleware at all. Because this commit created those endpoints in that unauthenticated state, it is the commit that introduced the vulnerability.
evidence: Fix commit `ea937d100956ca56689ff852d99c28589e2a7d88` adds `validateSessionMiddleware` to `packages/sync-server/src/app-simplefin/app-simplefin.js`, indicating missing auth was the issue.
BIC `e7f6348d9ae7d35dcfe4aa529a7eac09287c194e` newly adds `src/app-simplefin/app-simplefin.js` with routes `/status`, `/accounts`, and `/transactions` and no authentication middleware.
Search at the BIC found no use of `validateSessionMiddleware` in the SimpleFIN app, while the later codebase contains that middleware in `util/middlewares.js` and uses it in the fix.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: This commit (e7f6348d) introduced the SimpleFIN bank sync endpoints (`/status`, `/accounts`, `/transactions`) for the first time, creating the `app-simplefin/app-simplefin.js` file. Unlike the existing GoCardless app which imported and used `validateUser` for authentication, the SimpleFIN app was created without any authentication middleware or user validation. The fix commit (ea937d1) adds `validateSessionMiddleware` to these exact endpoints. Since this commit is where the unauthenticated SimpleFIN endpoints were first created, it is the direct cause of the missing authentication vulnerability (CWE-306).
evidence: The BIC creates app-simplefin.js with express routes (/status, /accounts, /transactions) but no authentication middleware or user validation
The existing GoCardless app at the same commit imports and uses `validateUser` for authentication, showing the pattern was known
The fix commit adds `validateSessionMiddleware` to the SimpleFIN app, directly addressing the missing authentication
The app is mounted at `/simplefin` in app.js without any parent-level authentication middleware

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The vulnerability (CVE-2026-27584) involves missing authentication for the SimpleFIN and Pluggy AI bank sync endpoints. The fix commit resolves this by adding `validateSessionMiddleware` to both `app-simplefin.js` and `app-pluggyai.js`. The suspected bug-introducing commit (`e7f6348d9ae7d35dcfe4aa529a7eac09287c194e`) is the initial implementation of the SimpleFIN integration. It creates `app-simplefin.js` and exposes endpoints like `/status`, `/accounts`, and `/transactions` without applying any authentication middleware (such as `validateUser`, which was used in other modules at the time). Therefore, this commit directly introduced the missing authentication vulnerability for the SimpleFIN endpoints.
evidence: The vulnerability is missing authentication for SimpleFIN and Pluggy AI endpoints.
The fix commit adds `validateSessionMiddleware` to `app-simplefin.js` and `app-pluggyai.js`.
The suspected BIC `e7f6348d9ae7d35dcfe4aa529a7eac09287c194e` introduces `app-simplefin.js` and its endpoints (`/status`, `/accounts`, `/transactions`) without any authentication middleware.
Other apps like `app-gocardless.js` used `validateUser` at the time, but it was omitted in `app-simplefin.js`.

bug_commits.author: Tom Crasset
bug_commits.message: integrate FileService for app-sync.js (#432)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit refactored the synchronization logic and introduced the `FilesService` and `verifyFileExists` patterns, but failed to implement any ownership or access control checks (like `requireFileAccess`) for the file operations. While it correctly identified if a file existed, it allowed any authenticated user (or in some cases, unauthenticated requests depending on middleware) to interact with any file ID, which is the root cause of the missing authorization vulnerability addressed in the fix.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The SimpleFIN and Pluggy AI bank synchronization endpoints were accessible without proper authentication, allowing unauthorized access to sensitive financial integration features.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: Missing authentication middleware or decorators on bank synchronization API routes
llm_verdict.causal_chain: 1. The blamed commit introduced a new file management service (FilesService) and refactored the /sync and file-related endpoints. 2. It implemented existence checks via verifyFileExists but omitted any logic to verify if the requesting user had permission to access the specific fileId. 3. This created a vulnerability where valid file IDs could be accessed or synchronized by unauthorized users, which was later fixed by adding requireFileAccess checks in the fix commit.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: Fallback verdict: API error on turn 1: [Errno 9] Bad file descriptor

This commit rewrote app-sync to use FilesService but preserved only existence/consistency checks and did not enforce per-file authorization on sensitive endpoints such as /sync, /user-get-key, /user-create-key, /reset-user-file, and /upload-user-file. The later fix adds a new requireFileAccess check on exactly these routes, showing the missing access control was the security fix. Since cc347aef introduced the vulnerable app-sync implementation in this service layer without those checks, it is the bug-introducing commit for this CWE-306 issue.
evidence: At cc347aef, src/app-sync.js has validateUserMiddleware globally, but the endpoints only call verifyFileExists(...) and then proceed; there is no owner/shared-access/admin check before returning keys, syncing, resetting, or uploading files.

The fix commit adds requireFileAccess(file, res.locals.user_id) and inserts it into /sync, /user-get-key, /user-create-key, /reset-user-file, and /upload-user-file, returning 403 on unauthorized access.
cc347aef specifically introduced/refactored app-sync.js to FilesService-based file lookups, replacing earlier direct SQL checks, yet still omitted any per-file authorization logic.
The fixed code also relies on file.owner and UserService.countUserAccess(...), indicating the missing control was access-to-file authorization, not mere session validation.

cves.description: Terraform Provider Debug Logs Vulnerable to Sensitive Information Exposure
cves.how_introduced: The Terraform provider failed to mark sensitive fields (such as database credentials or access controls) as 'Sensitive' in the resource schema, causing their values to be logged in plaintext within Terraform debug logs (TF_LOG).

bug_commits.author: Erik Zilber
bug_commits.message: Project: Private Image Sharing (#2170)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit introduces the exact vulnerable logging statement that was modified in the fix commit. It logs the entire 'createOpts' struct, which contains sensitive information like 'Token', leading to the sensitive information exposure.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The Terraform provider failed to mark sensitive fields (such as database credentials or access controls) as 'Sensitive' in the resource schema, causing their values to be logged in plaintext within Terraform debug logs (TF_LOG).
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: schema.StringAttribute{ ... Sensitive: false ... }
llm_verdict.causal_chain: The blamed commit added the 'Create' function for the resource, which included a 'tflog.Debug' call that logged the entire 'createOpts' object. Because 'createOpts' contains sensitive fields (like 'Token'), this caused sensitive information to be exposed in the Terraform debug logs.

bug_commits.author: Lena Garber
bug_commits.message: DBaaS/VPC: Implementation (#2089)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit introduces the 'PrivateNetwork' field and its associated logic, but the fix commit is specifically addressing the missing 'HostStandby' field. Furthermore, the vulnerability description identifies a failure to mark fields as 'Sensitive' in the schema, whereas both the blamed and fix commits only modify the data models and parsing logic, not the schema definitions where the 'Sensitive' property would be set.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The Terraform provider failed to mark sensitive fields (such as database credentials or access controls) as 'Sensitive' in the resource schema, causing their values to be logged in plaintext within Terraform debug logs (TF_LOG).
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: schema.StringAttribute{ ... Sensitive: false ... }

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The fix commit shows the security-relevant change was adding a new `host_standby` field while keeping `host_secondary` only as a deprecated compatibility alias. In the blamed file at the suspected introducing commit, the model exposed `host_secondary` as a normal Terraform attribute and populated it from database host data; this is the vulnerable behavior because Terraform provider debug logs can include attribute values, causing sensitive endpoint information to be logged under the misleading legacy field. Since the suspected commit is the one that introduced this framework model and the `host_secondary` attribute population into the relevant codepath, it is the origin of the vulnerable logging surface later corrected by the fix.
evidence: Fix commit message: "Add `host_standby` attribute and deprecate `host_secondary` attribute" indicates `host_secondary` was the problematic field.
At commit c5e4e0d58d93926f5039e96677db5739be744577, `DatabaseModel` includes `HostSecondary types.String   `tfsdk:"host_secondary"`` and `parseDatabases` sets it from `db.Hosts.Secondary`.

The fix for the same file adds `HostStandby types.String   `tfsdk:"host_standby"`` and starts populating the new field, showing the remediation was to replace/deprecate the old exposed attribute rather than unrelated code changes.

cves.description: Dify has a user enumeration issue
cves.how_introduced: The application likely returned different responses or errors depending on whether a user existed, allowing attackers to enumerate valid users. The fix introduces an exception counter to standardize error handling or limit failures.

bug_commits.author: -LAN-
bug_commits.message: feat: knowledge pipeline (#25360)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit introduced the initial implementation of the GraphExecution and GraphExecutionState classes without a mechanism to track the number of exceptions. The fix commit adds the exceptions_count field to these classes to mitigate the vulnerability.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The application likely returned different responses or errors depending on whether a user existed, allowing attackers to enumerate valid users. The fix introduces an exception counter to standardize error handling or limit failures.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: Observable response discrepancy based on user existence or unhandled exceptions leaking state.
llm_verdict.causal_chain: The blamed commit created the graph execution state management without tracking exception counts. This lack of tracking allowed an unbounded number of exceptions or specific error states to be observed, which the fix addresses by introducing exceptions_count and a method to record node failures.

cves.description: Agentgateway is missing parameter sanitization in MCP to OpenAPI conversion
cves.how_introduced: The Agentgateway failed to sanitize and encode parameters when converting MCP requests to OpenAPI calls, potentially allowing path traversal or header injection via unencoded path segments and unrestricted header propagation.

bug_commits.author: Eitan Yarmush
bug_commits.message: feat: Allow loading OpenAPI schemas from remote URLs [TESTING GOOGLE JULES AGENT] (#136)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit (dab07afea91e) refactored the OpenAPI parsing logic, moving it into a new structure where the `path` field was initialized using `path.clone()` without any sanitization or percent-encoding logic. This introduced the vulnerable pattern identified in the analysis, as the previous implementation (or the lack of this specific structure) did not expose this unvalidated path propagation path.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The Agentgateway failed to sanitize and encode parameters when converting MCP requests to OpenAPI calls, potentially allowing path traversal or header injection via unencoded path segments and unrestricted header propagation.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: path.clone() // without percent-encoding or validation of template parameters
llm_verdict.causal_chain: The commit introduced the `UpstreamOpenAPICall` struct and the `parse_openapi_schema` function, which directly assigned `path: path.clone()` to the struct without applying percent-encoding or validation, thereby creating the injection vulnerability.

cves.description: MobSF Vulnerable to Arbitrary File Write (AR-Slip) via Absolute Path in .a Extraction
cves.how_introduced: The application failed to properly validate user-supplied filenames, allowing attackers to use absolute paths or traversal sequences to access files outside the intended directory.

bug_commits.author: Ajin Abraham
bug_commits.message: June 22nd 2025 updates (#2530)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit introduced the 'get_script_content' function and the exact vulnerable line where 'is_safe_path' is called without the necessary third argument to properly validate the user-supplied script name.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The application failed to properly validate user-supplied filenames, allowing attackers to use absolute paths or traversal sequences to access files outside the intended directory.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: os.path.join(base_dir, user_input) without sufficient validation of user_input for absolute paths or traversal sequences
llm_verdict.causal_chain: The blamed commit created the file and added the 'get_script_content' function, which constructs a file path using user input ('script') and validates it using 'is_safe_path(str(others), str(script_file))'. Because it omitted the user input itself from the validation check, it allowed path traversal, leading to the vulnerability.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The fix changes the safety check in `get_script_content` from `is_safe_path(root, candidate)` to `is_safe_path(root, candidate, script)`, indicating the previous two-argument check was insufficient for attacker-controlled script names. The suspected commit is the one that introduced `mobsf/DynamicAnalyzer/views/common/frida/views.py` and specifically added the vulnerable `get_script_content` logic using `script_file = others / f'{script}.js'` followed by the weaker `is_safe_path(str(others), str(script_file))`. Since this file and code path did not exist before this commit, this commit introduced the vulnerable behavior.
evidence: Fix diff only modifies the vulnerable line in `get_script_content`, adding the third `script` argument to `is_safe_path` as the security fix.

Commit `7e0355c51d78d9911b5f8722f306d182f9975c35` newly adds `mobsf/DynamicAnalyzer/views/common/frida/views.py` with `script_file = others / f'{script}.js'` and `if not is_safe_path(str(others), str(script_file)):`.
`is_safe_path` at that commit only accepted two parameters and merely compared normalized real paths, so the vulnerable check present in the new code came directly from this commit.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The BIC commit (7e0355c) refactored code from `mobsf/DynamicAnalyzer/views/common/frida.py` to `mobsf/DynamicAnalyzer/views/common/frida/views.py`. The old file already had the identical vulnerable pattern: calling `is_safe_path(others, script_file)` with only 2 arguments, which didn't check the raw user input for absolute paths. The vulnerability existed before this commit in the original `frida.py` file. The BIC merely moved the pre-existing vulnerable code to a new location without introducing or changing the vulnerability. The real root cause is the `is_safe_path` function itself not checking raw user input for absolute paths, which predates this commit.
evidence: The old file `frida.py` (deleted in the BIC) contained the same vulnerable call: `if not is_safe_path(others, script_file):` with 2 arguments
The new file `frida/views.py` (created in the BIC) has the identical vulnerable call: `if not is_safe_path(str(others), str(script_file)):` with 2 arguments
The fix adds a third `raw_file` parameter to `is_safe_path` and enhances `is_path_traversal` to check for absolute paths - this is a fix to the utility function that predates the BIC
The `is_safe_path` function at the BIC commit only did `os.path.realpath(os.path.normpath(...))` comparison, which was already insufficient before the refactor

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The vulnerability is an Arbitrary File Write (AR-Slip) during `.a` extraction. The fix commit addresses this by modifying the `ar_os` function in `mobsf/StaticAnalyzer/views/common/shared_func.py` to check for path traversal. The fix commit also happens to fix a second vulnerability (GHSA-ccc3-fvfx-mw3v) by updating the `is_safe_path` utility function and all its callers, including `mobsf/DynamicAnalyzer/views/common/frida/views.py`. The suspected Bug-Introducing Commit (BIC) merely introduced `mobsf/DynamicAnalyzer/views/common/frida/views.py` and has absolutely nothing to do with `.a` extraction or the AR-Slip vulnerability.
evidence: The vulnerability is AR-Slip in .a extraction (GHSA-9gh8-9r95-3fc3).
The fix commit 7f3bc086c028c1b50889cab8a15f7b59b7abdaf9 fixes this by adding a check in `ar_os` in `mobsf/StaticAnalyzer/views/common/shared_func.py`.

The fix commit also fixes another vulnerability (GHSA-ccc3-fvfx-mw3v) by updating `is_safe_path` in `mobsf/MobSF/utils.py` and updating all its callers, including `mobsf/DynamicAnalyzer/views/common/frida/views.py`.
The suspected BIC 7e0355c51d78d9911b5f8722f306d182f9975c35 introduced `mobsf/DynamicAnalyzer/views/common/frida/views.py`, which is unrelated to .a extraction.

cves.description: Mattermost Confluence Plugin has Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions
cves.how_introduced: The application failed to properly validate subscription data during deserialization, allowing malformed or inconsistent subscription types to be processed.

bug_commits.author: kshitij katiyar
bug_commits.message: Check Confluence access for subscription and config checks (#155)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit refactored the subscription handling logic and established the pattern of calling deserialization functions without passing the subscription type context. llm_verdict.causal_chain: The commit removed existing validation checks and implemented a direct call to deserialization functions (SpaceSubscriptionFromJSON/PageSubscriptionFromJSON) that lacked the necessary context to verify if the input data matched the intended subscription type, leading to the improper input validation vulnerability.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The security fix is the change that separates request-decoding errors from Confluence access validation and validates the JSON/body first, including enforcing that the body's subscription type matches the URL type. In the suspected commit, validateUserConfluenceAccess was moved to run before checking whether deserialization succeeded, so malformed or type-mismatched input could be processed as a zero-value subscription and incorrectly treated as an access problem instead of being rejected as bad input. That is precisely an improper check/order-of-checks for exceptional conditions, and the later fix corrects this in edit_subscription.go.
evidence: Fix commit changes edit_subscription.go to use a separate sErr from SpaceSubscriptionFromJSON/PageSubscriptionFromJSON and returns immediately on decode error before validateUserConfluenceAccess runs.

At the fixed revision, SpaceSubscriptionFromJSON/PageSubscriptionFromJSON now take subscriptionType and explicitly reject mismatches with errors like 'subscription type mismatch' and missing identifiers.
In commit 97dbaf6c2983438401316865881e1f9630ccad2c, validateUserConfluenceAccess was inserted before the existing 'if err != nil' decode-error check in handleEditChannelSubscription.

The vulnerable version at 97dbaf6 shows subscription parsed into 'subscription, err = ...', then Confluence access validated using possibly invalid/zero-value subscription data, and only afterward checks 'if err != nil' for decode failure.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The BIC (97dbaf6c) moved the `validateUserConfluenceAccess` call to after the JSON deserialization switch block but before the `if err != nil` error check, reusing the same `err` variable. This causes the JSON decode error to be overwritten when `validateUserConfluenceAccess` succeeds (setting `err = nil`), meaning malformed or incomplete request bodies (e.g., missing SpaceKey or PageID) could bypass error checking and proceed to create/edit subscriptions. The fix commit (eccd0fdb) directly addresses this by introducing a separate `sErr` variable for deserialization errors and adding explicit validation for empty required fields in the `FromJSON` functions. This is a textbook CWE-754 (Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions) introduced by the BIC's code restructuring.
evidence: In edit_subscription.go after the BIC, the code flow is: (1) switch assigns err from SpaceSubscriptionFromJSON/PageSubscriptionFromJSON, (2) validateUserConfluenceAccess overwrites err with its own result using 'statusCode, err = ...', (3) 'if err != nil' check now tests validateUserConfluenceAccess result instead of JSON decode result
The fix commit introduces a separate 'sErr' variable specifically to prevent the error shadowing: 'var sErr error' is used for deserialization while 'err' remains for other operations

Before the BIC, the code had the err check immediately after the switch block, so JSON decode errors were properly caught. The BIC moved validateUserConfluenceAccess between the switch and the err check, introducing the shadowing bug
The fix also adds validation in SpaceSubscriptionFromJSON and PageSubscriptionFromJSON for empty SpaceKey/PageID and subscription type mismatches, which were not checked before

bug_commits.message: Add a handler for AxiosError errors (#189)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit explicitly introduced the use of `util.inspect` with `{ depth: null }` in the error handling logic. This pattern causes the logger to recursively dump the entire error object, including sensitive internal fields like authentication tokens and account details, which was the specific root cause identified in the vulnerability analysis.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The application logs full error objects and account details using `util.inspect` with infinite depth or `JSON.stringify`, which inadvertently exposes sensitive information like bearer tokens and account numbers in the logs.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: console.log('...', inspect(error, { depth: null }));
llm_verdict.causal_chain: 1. The blamed commit replaces standard object logging with `inspect(error, { depth: null })` to improve debugging visibility. 2. This change forces the logger to expand all nested properties of the error object. 3. When an Axios or GoCardless error occurs, the expanded output includes sensitive request headers (bearer tokens) and account metadata. 4. These secrets are then written to the application logs in plaintext.

Since the CVE fix is specifically for GoCardless sensitive-data logging in packages/sync-server, this commit is not the origin of that vulnerable code.

The 652d75a commit message is 'Add a handler for AxiosError errors (#189)' and its diff adds inspect(error...) logging only in app-nordigen.
The fixing commit 97482a082d502887ef22514b93e35e4c67f4f30f targets packages/sync-server/src/app-gocardless/app-gocardless.js to stop logging full error objects containing sensitive data.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: This commit introduced the vulnerable logging pattern. It imported `inspect` from `util` and changed error logging from `console.log({ message: 'Something went wrong', error })` to `console.log('Something went wrong', inspect(error, { depth: null }))`, which recursively dumps the entire error object with no depth limit. For Axios errors, this includes request configuration containing bearer tokens and response data containing account numbers. The commit also added a new `isAxiosError` handler that logs `inspect(error.response.data, { depth: null })`. The fix commit directly reverses this pattern by replacing all `inspect()` calls with `error.message` and safe specific fields.

bug_commits.author: James Long
bug_commits.message: initial (open-source)

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The vulnerability is caused by the lack of validation on the `fileId` parameter, which allows an authenticated user to perform path traversal attacks by supplying a payload like `../../` in the `x-actual-file-id` header. The fix commit addresses this by enforcing a strict regex pattern on `fileId`. The suspected commit is the initial commit of the repository, which introduced the `app-sync.js` file containing the `/upload-user-file` and `/download-user-file` endpoints. In this commit, `fileId` is directly used in `path.join()` operations without any sanitization, directly introducing the path traversal vulnerability. Therefore, this commit is confirmed as the bug-introducing commit.
evidence: The vulnerability is an Authenticated Path Traversal (CWE-22) in Actual Sync Server.
The fix commit adds a regex validation `FILE_ID_PATTERN = /^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$/` to the `fileId` parameter in the `/upload-user-file` and `/download-user-file` endpoints.
The suspected BIC is the initial open-source commit of the repository.
In this commit, `app-sync.js` is introduced, containing the `/upload-user-file` and `/download-user-file` endpoints.
In these endpoints, `fileId` is extracted from the `x-actual-file-id` header and directly passed to `path.join(config.files, fileId)` without any sanitization or validation, allowing path traversal.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The initial open-source commit (dd541e5f) introduced the `/upload-user-file` and `/download-user-file` endpoints that take `fileId` from request headers and use it directly in file path construction (`join(config.files, fileId)`) without any validation. An authenticated attacker could supply a `fileId` containing path traversal sequences (e.g., `../../etc/passwd`) to read or write arbitrary files on the server. The fix commit adds a regex pattern (`/^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$/`) to validate that `fileId` contains only safe characters, preventing path traversal.
evidence: The initial commit introduces `app-sync.js` with `/upload-user-file` endpoint using `let fileId = req.headers['x-actual-file-id']` directly in `join(config.files, fileId)` for file extraction without validation
The `/download-user-file` endpoint similarly uses `fileId` from headers in `join(config.files, fileId)` to read files without path traversal protection
The fix adds `FILE_ID_PATTERN = /^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$/` and `isValidFileId()` validation to both endpoints, confirming the path traversal vulnerability existed from the initial code
No fileId validation existed in the original code - the only check was `if (!fileId)` for null/empty values

cves.description: RustFS has SourceIp bypass via spoofed X-Forwarded-For/Real-IP headers

bug_commits.author: Copilot
bug_commits.message: feat: enhance console separation with enterprise-grade security, monitoring, and advanced tower-http integration (#513)

llm_verdict.reasoning: The blamed commit is a massive refactoring and documentation update related to console and endpoint service separation. It does not contain the specific logic for IP header parsing or the vulnerable pattern identified in the fix commit.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The application trusted user-controlled HTTP headers (X-Forwarded-For/X-Real-IP) to determine the client's source IP address for policy evaluation, allowing attackers to spoof their IP address.
llm_verdict.vulnerable_pattern: header.get("x-forwarded-for").or_else(|| header.get("x-real-ip"))

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The vulnerability is that IAM SourceIp conditions for admin requests were evaluated using spoofable forwarding headers instead of the actual socket peer address. The fix for admin auth adds a new remote_addr parameter and passes it into get_condition_values so SourceIp can be derived from the real connection. In commit 29c004d9352a, rustfs/src/admin/auth.rs is present with validate_admin_request/check_admin_request_auth still calling the old get_condition_values(headers, cred) form, meaning this commit introduced the admin auth path without supplying a trusted remote address and thus created the vulnerable behavior in that code path.
evidence: At BIC 29c004d9352a, rustfs/src/admin/auth.rs calls get_condition_values(headers, cred) in check_admin_request_auth with no remote_addr input.
The fix commit changes admin/auth.rs specifically to thread remote_addr through validate_admin_request/check_admin_request_auth and call get_condition_values(headers, cred, None, None, remote_addr).

HEAD-era get_condition_values includes SourceIp = get_source_ip_raw(header, &remote_addr_s), showing SourceIp evaluation depends on both headers and remote_addr; without remote_addr, admin auth lacked the trusted peer address needed to prevent spoofed X-Forwarded-For/Real-IP bypass.

Commit 29c004d9352a appears to have introduced rustfs/src/admin/auth.rs as part of console/admin separation work; there was no prior diff for that file in the commit because it was newly added in that state.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The vulnerability fixed later was the failure to properly reject malformed or inconsistent subscription edit requests before using the parsed subscription. In the suspected commit, the code was changed to call validateUserConfluenceAccess(userID, ..., subscriptionType, subscription) immediately after the switch, before checking whether JSON decoding actually succeeded; because subscription and err were reused, malformed input could flow into access validation under unusual/error conditions. The later fix specifically separates decoding errors into sErr, validates them immediately, and only then proceeds, showing that this commit introduced the vulnerable ordering/error-handling bug.

At the fix commit, SpaceSubscriptionFromJSON/PageSubscriptionFromJSON were updated to take `subscriptionType` and return explicit errors such as `spaceKey is required`, `pageID is required`, and `subscription type mismatch`, indicating malformed/unexpected request states needed to be handled earlier and more strictly.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The BIC (97dbaf6c) moved the `validateUserConfluenceAccess` call to after the JSON deserialization switch statement, reusing the same `err` variable. This means when `validateUserConfluenceAccess` succeeds (returns nil error), it overwrites any deserialization error that was stored in `err`, causing the subsequent `if err != nil` check to be bypassed. This allows subscriptions with missing required fields (empty `PageID`, empty `SpaceKey`, or mismatched subscription types) to pass through without proper validation — an "Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions" (CWE-754). The fix uses a separate `sErr` variable and adds explicit field validation in the `FromJSON` functions.

The fix commit modifies these types to remove a sensitive 'key' field from general API responses, but this appears to be a separate security hardening measure or a fix for a different issue than the SSRF described.
llm_verdict.vuln_description: The application allows server-side requests to be initiated via websocket connections, leading to SSRF.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The fix commit is about service-account API response schema and payloads: it adds a missing `login` field, removes an incorrect `key` field from general responses, and adjusts create/regenerate/delete handlers accordingly. The CVE concerns insufficient access control on document/version comparison endpoints, and no comparison endpoints or authorization logic are touched by either the fix commit or the suspected introducing commit. The suspected commit merely introduced the service accounts API/types and does not appear to create or modify any document comparison/version-comparison access checks.

Suspected commit 7eec4e8 adds service account types, entities, manager, and `/api/service-accounts` routes; no document/version comparison routes or related authorization code are introduced there.

The blamed file `app/common/ServiceAccountTypes-ti.ts` is just a generated type-definition file for service account API responses, unrelated to access control for comparisons between documents and versions.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The BIC (7eec4e8) introduced the entire Service Accounts API, including the `ServiceAccountTypes-ti.ts` file that defined `ServiceAccountApiResponse` with a `key` field (the API key). The fix commit (f662eac) removes the `key` from `ServiceAccountApiResponse` and replaces it with `login`, ensuring API keys are only exposed in creation responses (`ServiceAccountCreationResponse`). The BIC also introduced the API endpoints that used these types, including the apikey regeneration endpoint that returned the key in a `ServiceAccountApiResponse` type. Since the BIC created both the type definitions and the endpoints that the fix modifies, it is the commit that introduced the vulnerable code.

agent_verdicts.reasoning: The fix for CVE-2026-24002 is the newly added validation that every shared/invited email must satisfy `isEmail(email)` before being accepted into `notFoundUserEmailDelta`; prior to that, arbitrary strings could be treated as invite targets. The suspected commit introduced service accounts whose logins deliberately use the nonstandard `.invalid` domain via `getUserByLogin(login, {manager}, 'service')`, which requires accepting strings that would fail normal email validation. That commit left the access-update path in `UsersManager` without any email validation, thereby enabling the vulnerable behavior the later fix closes. In other words, this commit created the feature pressure and code path that made malformed pseudo-emails acceptable until the fix explicitly validated them.
evidence: Fix commit adds `isEmail` import and rejects invalid emails in `UsersManager.ts` before adding unknown principals to `notFoundUserEmailDelta`.
At the vulnerable commit, the same block lacks any email validation and directly executes `notFoundUserEmailDelta[email] = role!;`.
Commit 7eec4e8d introduces service accounts and documents their login format as `xxxxxxxxxxxx@serviceaccounts.invalid`.

`ServiceAccountsManager.createServiceAccount()` in 7eec4e8d constructs `${uuid}@${Login.SERVICE_ACCOUNTS_TLD}` and calls `getUserByLogin(..., 'service')`, establishing acceptance of these pseudo-email identities.
